99 (charge) |aggressive = Yes |poisonous = Yes ([[venom]]) |attributes = demon |attack style = [[Melee]], [[Magic]], [[Ranged]] |attack speed = 6 |slayxp = |cat = Bosses |assignedby = |hitpoints = 625 |hitpoints2 = 650 |hitpoints3 = 650 |hitpoints4 = 675 |hitpoints5 = 625 |att = 300 |str = 190 |def = 90 |mage = 275 |range = 110 |attbns = 210 |strbns = 45 |amagic = 125 |mbns = 30 |arange = 120 |rngbns = 50 |dstab = 300 |dslash = 300 |dcrush = 60 |dmagic = 160 |dlight = 160 |dstandard = 160 |dheavy = 160 |elementalweaknesstype = |elementalweaknesspercent = |immunepoison = Not immune |immunevenom = Poisons instead |immunecannon = Immune |immunethrall = Not immune |freezeresistance = 100 |id = 14709 |leagueRegion = Varlamore }} }} The '''Doom of Mokhaiotl''', also referred to as '''Osto-Ayak''', is a giant insect-like, [[demonic]] [[boss]] found in the [[Ruins of Mokhaiotl]], which is accessed during [[The Final Dawn]] quest, deep beneath the [[Tlati Rainforest]]. The Doom is the game's first delve boss, which shares similarities to ''RuneScape 3''{{'}}s {{RSL|Bosses#Enrage|streaking mechanics}}; upon defeating the Doom, it will descend further underground, and chasing after it into deeper "delve levels" increases the difficulty of the fight, up to the deepest delve level of 8 which can be further repeated. The initial delve levels are stated by the boss's developers to be comparable to the [[The Gauntlet|Corrupted Gauntlet]], with the deepest delve levels requiring a good understanding of game mechanics and "the ability to stay cool when things get really, ''really'' tough".{{CiteNews|newsdate=23 July 2025|url=https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/varlamore-the-final-dawn-out-now?oldschool=1|title=Varlamore: The Final Dawn Out Now}} Defeating the Doom of Mokhaiotl at deeper delve levels increases the chance to receive its unique drops, some of which will only be rolled upon its defeat at certain delve levels, though general drops are consistent across all delve levels. After defeating the Doom, the player can choose to continue their pursuit or leave and claim their rewards. If the player descends into a deeper delve level and is killed by the Doom, they will forfeit all accumulated drops from that run. Claiming the rewards, dying to the boss, or teleporting out will reset the delve level back to 1. Attempting to delve deeper upon receiving a unique reward will prompt a warning message. A [[Scoreboard (Doom of Mokhaiotl)|scoreboard]] found in the lobby area leading up to the Doom can be found to the east side of the wall, recording the player and global records against the boss. ==History== Known to the [[Old Ones]] as Osto-Ayak, which translates to "Under-Creature", the origin of the demonic insectoid is not known. What is known is that it was in a deep slumber beneath the earth, and was inadvertently awoken when construction of [[Mokhaiotl]] was well underway by [[human]] and [[dwarf (race)|dwarven]] refugees, who fled the mainland during the [[God Wars]]. The Kuhu sacrificed themselves to give the builders time to escape.{{CiteText|text=Stone tablet (The Final Dawn)|quote=Mokhaiotl has fallen. We awoke something, deep beneath the archive. Now everything is lost. [...] Mokhaiotl may be gone, but we have to make sure something remains. Their last act was one of sacrifice. We have to honour them. We have to remember them.|loc=The Final Dawn}} While various measures had already been put in place to protect the knowledge that would have been stored in Mokhaiotl, the Doom made no attempt to leave the premises, where it would remain isolated for an unknown amount of time, since the cavern had been sealed off and protective measures put in place, though it would consume anything unfortunate enough to disturb it, as indicated with various remains and carcasses scattered across its habitat. Eventually, during the events of the Final Dawn, its rest is disturbed once again with the death of [[Metzli, Teokan of Ranul]], and it presumably consumes her corpse after the adventurer, [[Prince Itzla Arkan]], and [[Furia Tullus]] left Mokhaiotl, though it appears to have made no attempt to leave the surrounding area. ==Fight overview== {{Main|Doom of Mokhaiotl/Strategies}} As a delve boss, pursuing the Doom of Mokhaiotl into deeper delve levels will increase the difficulty of the fight, up to delve level 8. In its most basic form (delve level 1), the Doom attacks with ranged and magic similar to that of [[the Leviathan]]; orbs from a distance, with blue being magic and green being ranged, blocked by praying before impact and a melee attack if directly next to it. Additionally, it will also use a rock throw attack which disables protection prayers upon shattering, then launching a magic or ranged projectile. Around a minute into the fight, it will then prepare a shockwave attack that the player must counter by making use of the [[volatile earth]] around the area. As the player descends down the [[burrow hole]], the Doom of Mokhaiotl's health slightly increases; the rest of its stats stay the same. Additionally, new mechanics are added and existing mechanics are enhanced upon, making the fight more challenging. After clearing delve 8, the player is awarded a "deep delve" count, and players can continue descending further down. These subsequent levels are level 8 in theory, though the Doom's health is reduced to 625 and hazards from previous levels continue to persist which practically make it more difficult. Similar to [[Yama]], players who die fighting the Doom are sent back to the lobby area instead of their [[respawn point]], where their graves are also located. Players will have their reclamation fee reduced by 75% until they have 20 total delves, after which the standard death cost will apply. ==Transportation== * Players can use a [[Mokhaiotl waystone]] to teleport to the [[Ruins of Mokhaiotl]]. * Players can use [[Pendant of ates]] and teleport to the [[Kastori]] waystone and then run north-west. * Use a [[Quetzal whistle]] to fly to [[Tal Teklan]], and then run East to the Tonali Cavern. * Once inside the [[Crypt of Tonali]], players can search the chest to the west of the lift platform to collect two ancient teleporters to place in the cavern and shorten the speed to get back to the boss. The teleporters must be placed within 30 tiles of each other. ==Money making== {{Mmgsection|Killing The Doom of Mokhaiotl (Delve 1-8)|Killing The Doom of Mokhaiotl (Delve 1-16)}} ==Drops== '''{{Colour|red|The quantities for all drops below, aside from uniques, are shown for delve level 3, due to the drop table's unique nature. See the [[#Quantity multiplier|table]] in the "Mechanics" section to find how quantities vary with delve level.}}''' [[File:Doom of Mokhaiotl loot interface.png|thumb|right|300px|The Doom reward interface.]] After defeating the Doom of Mokhaiotl, it will burrow down into the next level. Upon interacting with the [[burrow hole]] the Doom leaves behind, players are shown a list of rewards and given the option to claim them and leave, or to risk them by continuing the pursuit. Similarly to the [[Fortis Colosseum]], unique drops become available after completing specific delve levels, and the rates of all eligible uniques have their drop rates increased as the player descends further down. If the player completes a delve but leaves without claiming their loot, a [[Chest (Doom of Mokhaiotl)|chest]] across from the [[Scoreboard (Doom of Mokhaiotl)|scoreboard]] can be searched to collect any unclaimed rewards. ===Mechanics=== Each delve level rolls once on the regular loot table (with additional guaranteed [[demon tear]]s at certain delve levels), modifying them by using the following equation: :Q_n = Q_3 + trunc\left( Q_3 \cdot M_n \right), where Q_n is the quantity received at delve n and M_n is the quantity multiplier as listed in the table below. trunc(x) removes anything after a decimal point, rounding up for negative numbers and down for positive ones. As such, delve levels modify most of the common drops by rolling for an item, choosing a quantity as if it were level 3, multiplying it by the quantity multiplier at the given delve level, truncating the product, then adding it to the original quantity. For example, a player is equally likely to receive 2, 3, or 4 dragon platelegs at delve level 3. Delve level 2 has a -0.35x quantity multiplier, which gives possible quantities of 2, 2, and 3, respectively, meaning there is a 2/3 chance to receive two sets and a 1/3 chance to receive three sets. For uniques, each one has a minimum delve level that must be completed in order for it to be rolled, with their drop rates increasing the deeper the delve level. If a unique drop is rolled (including Dom, if it has not been logged yet), a light will emerge from the [[burrow hole]], and attempting to descend deeper will result in the player being warned if they intend to risk the item. {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |+ !Delve level !Quantity

Level 3 gives 50 tears, and each subsequent delve gives 10 additional tears up to a maximum of 100 tears starting at level 8.}} {{DropsTableBottom}} ==Loot calculator== {{Calculator:Doom of Mokhaiotl loot}} To calculate personal completions, [[Calculator:Doom of Mokhaiotl Completions Calculator|see here]]. ==Combat Achievements== {{Sync}} {{Combat Achievements list|Doom of Mokhaiotl}} ==Gallery== Doom of Mokhaiotl concept art.png|Concept art of the Doom of Mokhaiotl, by [[Mod Jerv]]. Osto-Ayak early concepts.png|Mod Jerv's early concepts for the Doom of Mokhaiotl, taking heavy inspiration from {{Wp|aggressive mimicry}} in nature. Doom of Mokhaiotl arena concept art.png|Concept art of the arena, by [[Mod Jerv]]. Doom of Mokhaiotl artwork.jpeg|Official artwork of the Doom of Mokhaiotl, by Mod Jerv. ==Changes== {{Subject changes header}} {{Subject changes |date = 1 October 2025 |update = Interface Uplift Round 2 |change = [[Sunfire splinters]] have been removed from the Doom of Mokhaiotl's loot table. }} {{Subject changes |date = 3 September 2025 |update = Doom Combat Achievements |change = * [[Combat Achievements]] have been added for the Doom of Mokhaiotl. As criteria for the Doom's speed tasks were tracked on release, these were automatically completed upon opening the Combat Achievements interface. * The "Attack" option on all volatile earth is now hidden when the [[earthen shield]] spawns. * The "Attack" option on demonic larvae is now hidden until their spawn animation finishes. }} {{Subject changes |date = 20 August 2025 |update = More Doom Tweaks, Poll 84, & Summer Sweep Up Changes |change = * Standard attacks from the Doom after a melee punish will now take into account rock throw projectiles, ensuring that two styles will never overlap (i.e. a melee projectile when a magic projectile from the rock throw is inbound). This still means that there are two attacks of the same style inbound if the player melee punishes too early, thus missing the prayer will result in the full impact of both attacks. * Attacks that are nulled during the transition to the Doom's shielded form will now instead count as damage towards the shield, even if the damage came from a non-demonbane weapon. * A bug in which the Doom's burrowed form deals trample damage whilst zooming diagonally, even if the player is not standing within its path, has been fixed. * Rocks which fall when the Doom begins to burrow will no longer disappear when landing on acid blood. * Players will no longer get pushed out-of-bounds when the [[burrow hole]] spawns in a corner. * The burrow hole will no longer sparkle when a player receives a duplicate [[Dom|pet]] drop. }} {{Subject changes |date = 13 August 2025 |update = A PoH Recode & More Fixes |change = * Further altered the shape of the Ranged projectile to ensure it appears noticeably different to Melee. * Fixed a bug where valuable loot drops received from the Doom of Mokhaiotl did not broadcast to the clan chat. }} {{Subject changes |date = 8 August 2025 |type = hotfix |change = * The Doom's transition to the shielded phase is now triggered by his attack timer instead of on-hit. To avoid instant phase transitions, he will also wait for 2 attacks after the last orb phase before triggering the shield. * The Doom can no longer heal unintentionally after firing his beam. * Giant larvae will now stay in place when KO damage is incoming, to match the behaviour of small larvae. }} {{Subject changes |date = 7 August 2025 |type = hotfix |change = * The holy water effect should now be guaranteed when killing the boss during the melee punish phase. * To prevent a bug that allowed the Doom to start a melee punish phase before switching to the blue shield phase a few ticks later, the beam charge during the melee punish phase can no longer be interrupted by grub damage. * Larvae no longer despawn and persist into the car phase. * The [[surge potion]] cooldown timer now pauses between delve levels. * Fixed a bug that caused some projectile attacks to be nullified when the Doom switched from the melee punish to the blue shield phase. * Fixed a bug that caused larvae spawning in to have an expanded clickbox that could overlap with [[volatile earth]]. }} {{Subject changes |date = 6 August 2025 |type = hotfix |change = * Re-equipping the [[lightbearer]] between delve levels no longer causes special attack energy to resume regenerating. * Giant demonic larvae will no longer incur attack delays even when using demonbane weapons. }} {{Subject changes |date = 6 August 2025 |update = Varlamore & Summer Sweep-Up Combat Tweaks |change = * Loot: ** Elite clue scrolls have been added to the drop table, with a drop rate of 1/75 for delve levels 1 and 2, and a drop rate of 1/50 for delve levels 3+. ** The [[burrow hole]] will now glow yellow when a unique item has been rolled. ** Unclaimed loot from the burrow hole can now be retrieved from a chest in the lobby if players leave before claiming loot. The loot interface can now also be accessed from further away. * Special Beam Cannon/melee punish: ** The charging effects were slightly shrunken to reduce the chance of misclicking. ** Firing of the Special Beam Cannon has been delayed by one tick (0.6s). ** The standard attack delay has been removed if the player is about to perform a melee punish. ** Recurrent damage from a melee punish will now trigger the holy water effect if it results in the Doom's defeat. ** The holy water effect will now restore a flat amount of 32 Hitpoints, 24 prayer points, and 25% special attack energy. Previously, they would restore players to 49 health and prayer points if they were lower than that. * Demonic larvae: ** Triple larvae spawns at delve levels 8+ have been replaced with a single giant demonic larva that has doubled health, a larger explosion radius, and tripled penalties if allowed to reach the Doom. ** Larvae will now try to spawn further away from the boss in addition to spawning on the same side as the player. ** Melee larvae will now only appear during the shield phase. ** Ranged larvae will now be one-shot when attacking with [[holy water]] equipped ** Magic larvae will now be one-shot when attacking with the [[Eye of Ayak]], which also now grants the attack delay-removing effect given by demonbane weapons. * Shockwave: ** Permanent rocks from the Rock Throw attack will no longer appear if the player is struck by it whilst under the protection of the [[earthen shield]]. ** The centre tile of the earthen shield will now remove any [[acid blood]] in its path. * Other: ** The [[bone dagger]]'s [[special attack]] now correctly guarantees a hit against the Doom when used on the first hit: ** Special attack energy no longer regenerates when the Doom is not present. ** The current delve level now appears in the Doom's health overlay, if enabled. ** The Doom's clickbox has been adjusted to help prevent players from clicking through it when they intended to attack. ** Rock Throw attacks are now less likely to be used in succession. ** The colour of the melee projectiles has been adjusted to be clearer for players with visual impairments. ** The [[dwarven multicannon]] can no longer be placed in the delve level 1 area. }} {{Subject changes |date = 30 July 2025 |update = Varlamore: Bug Fixes & More |change = * [[Demonbane spell]]s now benefit from reduced attack delay on [[Demonic larva|demonic larvae]]. * A bug causing loot from previous waves to not always appear in the 'Investigate' interface has been fixed. * The [[clickbox]] for volatile earth nodes no longer appear before their spawn animation has finished. * [[Clan]] broadcasts will now only trigger for waves 1-8 for a player's personal best rather than on every completion. * The size of the beam charge-up effect has been reduced to help prevent misclicks. }} {{Subject changes |date = 29 July 2025 |update = Varlamore: The Final Dawn Out Now |type = hotfix |change = Rocks present underneath the boss when it performs its slam during the Burrow attack will now be destroyed after the boss has slammed. }} {{Subject changes |date = 24 July 2025 |update = Varlamore: The Final Dawn Out Now |type = hotfix |change = The protective effects offered by the [[earthen shield]] will now last one tick longer. }} {{Subject changes footer}} ==Development history== The Doom's rewards underwent multiple iterations and revisions due to player feedback. The following is a list of scrapped rewards: * [[Avernic catalyst]] * Consumable [[thrall]] upgrade item * [[Demon spade]] * [[Demonic mark]] * [[Diabolical aegis]] * [[Eldritch necklace]] * [[Eldritch veil]] ==Trivia== * The examine text is a reference to the [[demon spade]], a proposed reward from the Doom that was initially pitched as the "demonic digger" before being renamed. The spade was later scrapped. * The Doom's design was inspired by in-game demons, the {{WP|praying mantis}}, and {{WP|aggressive mimicry}}, a form of mimicry used by predators to lure in prey.{{CiteReddit|author=Mod Jerv|url=https://www.reddit.com/r/2007scape/comments/1mh7cal/comment/n6u4by5/|title=Osto-Ayak, the Doom of Mokhaiotl|quote=We wanted the design to be insectoid and demonic, and I tried a few different routes and sent them Mack’s way to see if any caught momentum and inspiration. I took inspiration from demons in game, a Praying Mantis and aggressive mimicry for the the final boss design! A lot of back and forth with animation to ensure the process of creating would be optimal.|date=4 August 2025|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/5xRUg|archivedate=4 August 2025}} * If the player receives multiple Dom pet drops in one delve, when loot is claimed, the [[collection log]] will only add 1 pet to the log instead of the actual amount received.{{CiteReddit|author=Gregkow|url=https://www.reddit.com/r/2007scape/comments/1mbze13/jagex_just_scammed_me_out_of_a_dom_pet_details_in/|title=Jagex just scammed me out of a Dom pet, details in post|date=28 July 2025|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/5u2D2|archivedate=29 July 2025}} * [[Dragon platelegs]] appear unnoted in the reward interface, but are withdrawn as [[bank note]]s. All other noted items appear as bank notes in the interface. * Similarly to [[Yama]], the Doom's killcount is counted differently; rather than kill count, it is "delves completed" and "deep delves completed". ** Unlike any other boss in ''Old School RuneScape'', the actual "kill count" is the amount of deep delves completed; because of this, the kill count will not show in the player's [[HiScores]] until they have completed five deep delves. Since all drops are available on delve 6 and deeper, it is possible to complete the entire log without ever getting a proper kill count for the boss, a trait that it shares with the [[Chaos Elemental]]. * The Doom had a festive override wearing a [[santa hat]] during the [[2025 Christmas event/Gallery|2025]] Christmas event. ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Doom of Mokhaiotl}} {{Bosses}} {{Demons}}
